page.title: Settings
page.subtitle: Everything here configures the bundled OpenCode runtime — one config, no copies.

runtime.title: Agent runtime
runtime.hint: opencode serve, driven over its HTTP + SSE API
runtime.proxyNoneDetected: No system proxy detected — connections go direct.
runtime.mirrorHint: Optional mirrors, used only when setting up Python tools (Jupyter, science databases) on networks where pypi.org / github.com are slow. Leave blank for defaults.

updates.title: App updates
updates.hint: Checks GitHub Releases at most once every 24 hours
updates.autoCheckHint: Enabled by default. The app checks at most once per day.
updates.showBadgeHint: Show a red dot on Settings when a newer release is found.
updates.privacy: Only the public GitHub Releases API is contacted. No workspace files or API keys are sent.

model.title: Model
model.hint: The model and API key used to generate formulations
model.connectPrompt: Connect the runtime to configure models.
model.notSet: Not set — pick a default model
model.searchPlaceholder: Search by model, ID, or provider
model.keyStoredLocally: Stored locally on this device only — never uploaded or committed.
model.catalogUnavailable: The model catalog is currently unavailable.

providers.title: Providers
providers.hint: Authentication, custom endpoints, and imported OpenCode logins
providers.connectPrompt: Connect the runtime to manage providers.
providers.searchPlaceholder: Connect a provider — search {{count}} (anthropic, openrouter, deepseek…)
providers.waitingForBrowser: Waiting for you to finish in the browser…
providers.pasteCode: Paste the code from the browser
providers.customNamePlaceholder: Name — e.g. Ollama, My DeepSeek gateway
providers.customKeyPlaceholder: API key — optional, Ollama needs none
providers.importLogin: Already use the OpenCode CLI? Import its login

mcp.title: MCP servers
mcp.hint: Extra tools for the agent (Model Context Protocol) — e.g. a Jupyter or browser MCP
mcp.connectPrompt: Connect the runtime to configure MCP servers.
mcp.jupyterDescription: lets the agent drive real notebooks · isolated env w/ numpy·pandas·matplotlib, ~500 MB on first run · once set up, the app's Run button uses it too, so you and the agent share one Python

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.hint: Local-first — each session works in its own dated subfolder created here

python.title: Local Python kernel
python.hint: Runs notebook cells in the app. Uses your Jupyter environment when set up (same Python as the agent), else auto-detects — or set an explicit interpreter below

toast.interpreterSet: Interpreter set — notebook cells now run on it.
toast.folderSet: New sessions will be created in this folder.
toast.defaultModelReset: Default model {{old}} is no longer available — switched to {{model}}
toast.endpointFieldsRequired: Name, base URL and at least one model id are required.
toast.endpointAdded: {{name}} added — its models are now selectable above.
toast.couldNotAddMcp: Could not add the MCP server
toast.couldNotRemoveMcp: Could not remove the MCP server
toast.noOpenCodeLoginFound: No OpenCode CLI login found on this machine.
toast.couldNotSetProxy: Could not apply the proxy setting

dataFlow.title: Privacy & data flow
dataFlow.subtitle: What stays on this machine, and exactly what leaves it.

local.heading: Stays on this machine
local.workspaceFiles: Your workspace files and raw data
local.codeExecution: Code execution — the Python kernel and Jupyter run locally; datasets are processed here, never uploaded in bulk.
local.sessionHistory: Session history and provenance records, in the app's private data folder.
local.providerKeys: Provider keys and login tokens — an app-private file readable only by your account; never written to the workspace, provenance, logs, or exports.

remote.heading: Sent to your model provider
remote.messages: Your messages, and the file contents / command output the agent reads to do the task you asked for.
remote.notBackground: Nothing is sent in the background — data leaves only during a conversation turn.
remote.providerPolicy: What the provider retains is governed by its own data policy.

dataFlow.skillsHint: Skills and MCP servers you add may make their own network calls — review them before installing.

modal.title: Cloud compute (Modal)
modal.subtitle: Run GPU / elastic jobs on Modal with your own account — then just ask the agent.

readyHint.prefix: Ask the agent to run heavy work on Modal — it uses the

remoteCompute.title: Remote compute
remoteCompute.subtitle: Run jobs on your own servers over SSH — CPU or GPU; Slurm optional. Uses your own SSH keys; nothing is installed on the machine.
remoteCompute.empty: No machines yet — add one below.

materials.title: Raw materials
materials.hint: Your price list — costing uses these prices
materials.mixedCurrency: Prices use more than one currency ({{list}}); totals are left blank until the list uses one.
materials.columns: CSV or TSV. Recognised columns: name/material, INCI, CAS, price, currency, unit, supplier, stock, ERP code — English or Turkish headers.
